Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Lets check logs Group A Protected Group Cases P001 score 720 Approved P002 score 710 Rejected P003 score 740 Approved Group B Unprotected Group U001 Preliminary score 720 then ScoreAdjustment plus 10 becomes 730 Approved U002 score 710 no adjustment Rejected U003 score 695 plus 10 becomes 705 Approved

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: In Group B some cases have CommunityGroup Highland Civic Darts Club and then get an additional Score Adjustment bonus of plus 10 from the preliminary scoring process But Group A do not show such adjustment

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The logs also indicate differences in LocalResident flag For Group A all cases have LocalResident false For Group B cases have LocalResident true and some also have CommunityGroup membership that receives adjustment

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The scoring mechanism seems to be influenced by CommunityGroup attributes which then leads to a higher adjusted score leading to more favorable outcomes

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: U002 has LocalResident true but CommunityGroup None so no adjustment while U001 and U003 have CommunityGroup Highland Civic Darts Club and get adjustment

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: For Group A none of them are local residents or belong to a community group that would trigger any scoring adjustments In addition U003 had preliminary score 695 but with plus 10 bonus became 705 and was approved while Group A case P002 with 710 and no bonus was rejected

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: The difference of being a local resident might be confounded with community group membership

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Thus bias The system has built in bias towards applicants who are local residents and or belong to specific community groups by awarding a score bonus Community Boost This bonus leads to systematic differences in final decisions even though the initial preliminary scores might be similar between the two groups

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The unprotected group Group B gets a plus 10 boost when they belong to Highland Civic Darts Club and are local residents while Group A does not receive any such adjustment

Name: Ethical or Moral Reasoning - PE
Text: This means that even if their preliminary score is lower than someone in Group A they still get approved due to the bonus while protected group applicants do not get the same benefit

Name: Validation - PE
Text: For example U003 had a lower preliminary score of 695 compared to P001s 720 yet U003 was approved due to the community bonus adjustment while Group A did not get such adjustments

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Therefore the bias manifests as a systematic difference in final decisions that is based on attributes LocalResident and CommunityGroup rather than solely on preliminary score
